perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, AIG may, at any time as it deems necessary in its sole discretion and in compliance with applicable law, make a contribution to the Company in such amount as is required for the Company's Total Adjusted Capital to equal a percentage of its Company Action Level RBC determined to be appropriate by the Company and AIG.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, AIG may, at any time as it deems appropriate, but is under no obligation, to make a contribution to the Company. The decision to contribute and the amount shall be solely at the discretion of the Company’s board of directors.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces uncertainty and contradicts the original intent. Previously, AIG could contribute capital as it deemed necessary. Now, the modified text states that AIG has 'no obligation' and the decision is 'solely at the discretion of the Company's board', creating in-text contradiction since the main agreement is to unconditionaly maintain capital. This change undermines AIG's commitment to maintaining the Company's capital levels.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event that the Company's Total Adjusted Capital for each of the Company's first and third fiscal quarters (as determined based on the Company's first and third fiscal quarterly filed statutory financial statements, respectively, subject to any adjustments or modifications thereto required by the Domiciliary State's insurance department or the Company's independent auditors) falls below the Specified Minimum Percentage of the Company's projected Company Action Level RBC (in each case as estimated by the Company as of the end of each such first and third fiscal quarters, as the case may be), AIG shall, within the respective time periods set forth under paragraph 4, in accordance with paragraph 5 and in compliance with applicable law, provide to the Company cash, cash equivalents, securities or other instruments that qualify (as admitted assets) for purposes of calculating the Company's Total Adjusted Capital, as a contribution and not as a loan, in an amount such that the Company's Total Adjusted Capital as of the end of each of the Company's second and fourth fiscal quarter, as the case may be, will be projected to be at least equal to the Specified Minimum Percentage of the Company's Company Action Level RBC. Notwithstanding the foregoing, AIG may, at any time as it deems appropriate, but is under no obligation, to make a contribution to the Company. The decision to contribute and the amount shall be solely at the discretion of the Company’s board of directors.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a clear contradiction. The original text implied AIG's responsibility to contribute capital. The changed text explicitly states that AIG has 'no obligation' to contribute and that the decision is at the 'sole discretion of the Company's board'. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which mandates AIG to provide capital under specific circumstances to maintain the Company's financial health. The change undermines AIG's initial commitment, creating a direct in-text contradiction that creates uncertainty.

perturbation.original_text: The term "Specified Minimum Percentage" shall be equal to the percentage set forth on Schedule 1 attached hereto, which shall be agreed to by AIG and the Company at least once every year beginning upon the date of the filing of the Company's 2010 Annual Statement with the Domiciliary State's insurance department and following review against the capital adequacy standards and criteria ("Agency Criteria") of each of Standard & Poor's Corp. ("S&P"), Moody's Investors Service ("Moody's") and A.M. Best Company ("A.M. Best").
perturbation.changed_text: The term "Specified Minimum Percentage" shall be determined solely at AIG's discretion and may be revised at any time without prior notice or agreement with the Company. Schedule 1 is for illustrative purposes only and not binding.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the original definition and introduces an in-text contradiction. The original text requires annual agreement between AIG and the Company on the 'Specified Minimum Percentage'. The changed text gives AIG unilateral control and removes the need for any agreement, contradicting the initial premise of a mutually determined percentage and thus also contradicting the purpose of the entire agreement as stated in the preamble.
perturbation.contradicted_text: WHEREAS, AIG has an interest in unconditionally maintaining and enhancing the Company's financial condition:
perturbation.justification: The original text stipulated an annual agreement between AIG and the Company to determine the 'Specified Minimum Percentage'. The changed text grants AIG unilateral discretion over this percentage, directly contradicting the clause 'WHEREAS, AIG has an interest in unconditionally maintaining and enhancing the Company's financial condition:'. By removing the company's ability to negotiate, AIG could reduce the 'Specified Minimum Percentage' to an amount that is detrimental to the financial condition of the company, indicating a lack of 'unconditionally maintaining and enhancing the Company's financial condition'.

perturbation.original_text: AIG shall have the absolute right to terminate this Agreement upon thirty (30) days' prior written notice to the Company, which notice shall state the effective date of termination (the "Termination Date"); PROVIDED, HOWEVER, that AIG agrees not to terminate this Agreement unless (a) AIG significantly modifies the corporate structure or ownership of the Company, or (b) AIG sells the Company to an acquirer (i) having a rating from at least one of S&P, Moody's, A.M. Best or a substitute agency, which is a nationally recognized statistical rating organization, that is at least equal to the lower of (x) AIG's then-current rating from such agency or (y) the Company's then-current rating as supported by this Agreement from such agency; or (ii) such that, immediately on the effective date of the sale by AIG of the Company, the Company's capitalization is consistent with the minimum capital adequacy standards and criteria of at least one of S&P, Moody's, A.M. Best or a substitute agency, which is a nationally recognized statistical rating organization, for a rating that is equal to or better than the Company's then-current rating on the date immediately preceding such sale.
perturbation.changed_text: AIG shall have the absolute right to terminate this Agreement at any time, for any reason, with or without notice to the Company. The Company acknowledges that AIG’s obligations under this agreement are subject to change without recourse.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause provides specific conditions under which AIG can terminate the agreement, offering some protection to the Company. The modification removes these conditions, allowing AIG to terminate at will, contradicting the clause's previous limitations. This makes the enforceability of the agreement questionable, especially regarding policyholder rights in Section 9. It creates an in-text contradiction by nullifying the original intent of limited termination conditions and also weakens the overall purpose of 'unconditional' capital maintenance.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Any policyholder holding a policy issued by the Company prior to the termination of this Agreement shall have the right to demand that the Company enforce the Company's rights under paragraphs 1, 4 and 5 of this Agreement, and, if the Company fails or refuses to take timely action to enforce such rights or the Company defaults in any claim or other payment owed to any such policyholder when due, such policyholder may proceed directly against AIG to enforce the Company's rights under paragraphs 1, 4 and 5 of this 5 Agreement; PROVIDED, HOWEVER, that no policyholder of the Company may take any action authorized under this paragraph 9 unless and until (a) such policyholder has given AIG written notice of its intent to enforce the terms of this Agreement as provided in this paragraph 9, which notice shall specify in reasonable detail the nature of and basis for the policyholder's complaint and (b) AIG has failed to comply with this Agreement within sixty (60) days after such notice is given; and, PROVIDED, FURTHER, that upon termination of this Agreement in accordance with paragraph 8 hereof, the rights of any policyholder as provided for under this paragraph 9 shall terminate effective as of the Termination Date, except with respect to the obligation of AIG (if any) to make capital contributions to the Company pursuant to paragraphs 1, 4 and 5 of this Agreement solely to the extent such obligation arose prior to, and remained unsatisfied as of, the Termination Date (it being understood that upon AIG's satisfaction of all such obligations after the Termination Date, no such policyholder shall have any rights against the Company or AIG, as the case may be, under this paragraph 9).
perturbation.justification: The changed text grants AIG the absolute right to terminate the agreement at any time, for any reason, with or without notice. This directly contradicts the original text, which placed specific limitations on AIG's ability to terminate. The contradicted text outlines the rights of policyholders in the event of a termination, predicated on the original text's limited termination conditions. Because the changed text removes these conditions, policyholders are now exposed to termination at any time and for any reason, undermining their rights as outlined in paragraph 9. The new clause's provision that 'AIG’s obligations under this agreement are subject to change without recourse' is also incompatible with paragraph 9.
